content: We can reframe hierarchical task planning’s fixed top-down task decomposition structure to dynamically grow its task hierarchy *from the bottom up*, guided by an ICM curiosity mechanism that allocates deeper, more granular task branches only to high-uncertainty regions of the state space that the agent has not yet explored. This creates a self-organizing planning hierarchy that adapts its depth and breadth to the agent’s current knowledge gap, rather than being pre-specified by human designers.
